strengths: Direct experience contributing to AI-agent infrastructure and developer tooling at re:cinq, including MCP and agentic frameworks, aligning with the CTO role’s focus on modular, event-driven multi-agent systems.

Proven backend and distributed-systems expertise demonstrated at Dunnhumby (microservices handling millions of HTTP requests/sec) and Sociomantic (low-latency, real-time metadata apps), which is directly relevant to fintech-scale agentic AI systems.

Strong track record of scaling engineering teams and culture, including mentoring, TDD/pairing practices at RIDE Capital, and structuring dev-ops infrastructure (GitLab CI, Docker, Artifactory), matching the leadership requirements for high-performance teams.
concerns: Limited explicit fintech experience outside of pricing optimization at Optilyz, though the role’s requirement for fintech familiarity may still be met with demonstrated infrastructure and scaling expertise.
No direct mention of regulatory/compliance infrastructure design in past roles, a key responsibility for fintech CTOs.
Current role at re:cinq is recent (April 2025), leaving little long-term visibility into leadership or system-scale outcomes.
response.justification: Bogdan’s hands-on contributions to agentic AI infrastructure (MCP, agentic frameworks) at re:cinq and his proven distributed-systems chops (microservices, real-time systems) at Dunnhumby/Sociomantic directly address core architectural needs for agentic AI and fintech integration. His leadership in scaling teams (RIDE Capital, Altom) and building dev-ops culture aligns with the CTO’s mandate for disciplined, high-velocity engineering. While fintech-specific experience is thinner, his infrastructure depth and rapid adaptability to new domains (e.g., MCP) suggest high upside for the role’s demands.
